entry.title: Enjoy Solitude
entry.content:
Just learn to live a healthy and productive life…

2020 is a special year, in all it’s perspectives…

I think it’s time for me to think about how to plan my life, to find better balance, I admit that I feel very uncomfortable when suddenly start to work from home, because I can’t master the “freedom”… without the work environment full of colleagues… It’s very easy to enter the disrupted mode.
entry.summary: Just learn to live a healthy and productive life…

entry.title: Take Your Sweet Time...
entry.content:
The most important lesson I learned in this year.

认识到自己的普通, 但不要失去自信… 这之中的秘密就是努力做好小事的能力, 它会带给一个人高贵的气质…

请选择和时间做朋友… THINK BIG, THINK LONG…

Savor your life, don’t worry. The common sense will become more and more clear… Believe me, it’s not easy.

“You don’t have a lot of envy.”
“You don’t have a lot of resentment.”
“You don’t overspend your income.”
“You stay cheerful in spite of your troubles.”
“You deal with reliable people.”
“You do what you’re supposed to do.”
And all these simple rules work so well to make your life better. And they’re so trite.

from Charlie Munger

……

“Do the thing you fear and the death of fear is certain.”

from Ralph Waldo Emerson

……

Nothing in this world can take the place of persistence.
Talent will not; nothing is more common than unsuccessful men with talent.
Genius will not; unrewarded genius is almost a proverb.
Education will not; the world is full of educated derelicts.
Persistence and determination alone are omnipotent.

from Calvin Coolidge

……

Your security rests in knowing how to do a variety of things, and being able to do them better than others.

from Lloyd Blankfein

In the past, I know that we should make friends with excellent people, but I still underestimate the influence, actually I think your social connections build you, means you always implicitly mimic your friend’s behavior. Let’s pursue good things, and the only one principle… “Learn by doing things”…
entry.summary: The most important lesson I learned in this year.

entry.title: Psychological Maturity...
entry.content:
Just take the courage to confront all the difficulties in life.

Here is an image, which illustrates this very idea… Love it.

Remarkable year for me, the most important thing is that I regain confidence… More than that, I become more psychologically mature than before. Life is full of uncertainty, I can’t make precise prediction… But for me, personally speaking, really a significant year.

This article is very good for me… The name of it is 20 Signs You’re Emotionally Mature.

Tips
You realize people are never good or bad, they are traumatized and complex. Complexity is interesting.
You learn to articulate your emotions to get your point across.
You acknowledge that you are not perfect and learn to apologize.
You learn to be confident without being narcissistic.
You forgive your parents for fucking you up so, because you keep remembering they have gone through the same.
You make important decisions and initiate important conversations only when you are completely rested from various indirect stressors.
You forgive people who hurt you, even if they don’t understand after your explain why you were hurt.
You cease to believe in perfect life and perfect people.
You don’t keep your hopes up too high in general and learn to be comfortable with it.
You understand that other people’s weaknesses are always balanced by their strengths and learn to concentrate on them.
You don’t fall in love for superficial qualities.
You understand that you are just as big pain in the ass to be around with, as the other people seem to be.
You learn to forgive yourself regardless of how you suck at everything.
You make peace with an immature version of yours that shows itself once in a while.
You seek satisfaction in tiny achievements rather than in monumental ones.
You stop caring what others think of you.
You appreciate healthy criticism.
You make time to examine your life.
You are able to identify inner traumas you received as a child and avoid acting on impulses instilled by those experiences.
You understand that true friendship is about exchanging a personal story, rather than best advice.

Want to share with you guys… The main focus, I notice in this article, is that how can I become more responsible for both work and life.

Time again, I find that I should insist on the habit of reflection and gratitude. Because if you lose this trait, you will become arrogant very quickly… Remind myself, of being grateful for what happened…

From another perspective, if I can fully understand the essence of psychological maturity… I can behave according the following tips… 40 Signs You’ve Finally Learned To Love Yourself.

Tips
You’re not afraid to ask for what you want and you are not afraid to say what you don’t want.
You believe in yourself and you know that you are capable of achieving what you truly desire.
You hone your talents and your strengths and you don’t need external motivation to keep you going.
You stopped giving chances to people who keep letting you down.
You’ve moved on from toxic relationships and you are not looking back.
You started filtering your inner circle and detached yourself from the negative people and the ones who do not truly care about you.
You defend yourself and your ideas to those who belittle you.
You stand up for yourself when you need to instead of biting your tongue to please people.
You’re actively going to the gym and taking care of your body for yourself not for anyone else.
You’re crossing things off your bucket list and adding more incredible items.
You are spending more time doing things that you are passionate about.
You’re making new connections with people who inspire you.
You are closing old doors that lead to nowhere and you are finding new doors that lead to better places.
The voices in your head are a lot kinder and more supportive than they used to be.
You forgave yourself for your mistakes and tried to see the lessons in them.
You are more confident in yourself and in your abilities even when people are doubting you.
You know when to walk away from people who don’t appreciate you.
You are truly enjoying your life now and you are grateful for it.
You are more assertive in your decisions and in your feelings.
You are being real to the people around you and to yourself.
You are not afraid of saying sorry or apologizing.
You do not take rejection personally and you understand it is a part of life.
You put yourself first when you need to.
You don’t save things for special occasions or for certain people. You want to look and feel good all the time.
You are not afraid of shining or promoting yourself (in a healthy way.)
You genuinely feel happy for other people and you stopped comparing yourself to others.
You wake up every day ready to do something wonderful.
You stopped saying negative statements like ‘I’m a mess’ or ‘I’m not good enough.’
You turned your negative self-talk into positive affirmations.
You are calmer and you stopped letting the small things get to you.
You are becoming more spiritual and trusting the universe to bring you everything you want.
You respect yourself and you no longer tolerate people who don’t.
You know that your voice is important and it matters even if people don’t agree.
You are making plans and pursuing interests without waiting for someone to join you.
You’ve become the voice of optimism to your friends and family.
You want to learn more and are always looking for ways to be better a person.
You do not chase people or love or anything that obliges you to degrade yourself.
You smile more than you cry and you finally like your reflection in the mirror.
You no longer feel lonely because you actually like the person you’re alone with.
You believe that you deserve phenomenal love and you know you will find it.

When you really understand these invaluable principles… Then you can enjoy life much more…

时刻想着, 并不断问自己一个问题, “有限的精力, 有限的生命, 我应该坚持哪些事, 放弃哪些事, 才不枉世上走一遭?”
entry.summary: Just take the courage to confront all the difficulties in life.

entry.title: Self-Check Card, Systems Design
entry.content:
Master all the basic systems design principles, they are unescapable, and really helpful in your day to day work…

…The only thing I can do is to try to solve as many as problems, and analyze them thoroughly, case by case, and repetitively…

Let’s speed up the learning process!

No.Quiz
1Pastebin.com(or Bit.ly)
entry.summary: Master all the basic systems design principles, they are unescapable, and really helpful in your day to day work…

entry.title: Self-Check Card, Algorithms
entry.content:
Master all the basic algorithms, they are unescapable.

Problems those I can’t come up with solutions directly… Need to rethink them.

entry.title: Algorithm Analysis, Learning Report
entry.content:
I need note this, by writing the mathematical formulas by hands, I can retain them.

The best way to master these materials is through analyzing the real problems, and think them repetitively.

An illustration image of asymptotic notations involved in discussion.

Here, I need review the three notations in the asymptotic analysis of algorithms,

First, - notation

Definition:

Second, - notation

Definition:

Third, - notation

Definition:

Other notations in lectures,

Fourth, - notation

Definition:

Another popular way to illustrate this,

Fifth, - notation

Definition:

Another popular way to illustrate this,

Great way to draw an analogy between the asymptotic comparsion of two functions and and comparison of two real numbers and :
entry.summary: I need note this, by writing the mathematical formulas by hands, I can retain them.

entry.title: Linux Command Page
entry.content:
Find a good illustration of linux command, remember refer this when forgot.

Here is a good manual for linux command usage.
entry.summary: Find a good illustration of linux command, remember refer this when forgot.

entry.title: 6.824 Distributed Systems
entry.content:
Case study is the core of this course, means many industrial level production distributed systems…

No.Paper
1MapReduce: Simplified Data Processing on Large Clusters
2The Google File System
3The Design and Evaluation of a Practical System for Fault-Tolerant Virtual Machines
4In Search of an Understandable Consensus Algorithm (Extended Version)
5Using Paxos to Build a Scalable, Consistent, and Highly Available Datastore
entry.summary: Case study is the core of this course, means many industrial level production distributed systems…

entry.title: 6.S081 Operating System Engineering
entry.content:
Being a craftsman… Only way to learn to do systems…

By following this course, I want to sum up the important concepts in operating systems field…

Theory Note, less is more!
entry.summary: Being a craftsman… Only way to learn to do systems…

entry.title: 15-213 Introduction to Computer Systems
entry.content:
Excellent opportunity to explore computer systems…

Labs are the core of this class, at the meantime, I should also catch up with readings, remember the only way to learn system is through doing system.

Chapter 1: A Tour of Computer Systems

I encounter a very good image to illustrate the general abstractions in computer systems…

Chapter 2: Representing and Manipulating Information

Some tips gained from this chapter,

  • Think about how to implement XOR: x ^ y = (x & ~y) | (~x & y), if only permit using &, ~, how about the solution?
  • 将小范围有符号数转为大范围无符号数时，C标准要求先改变范围，再改变符号！
  • How C language treat that the number convertion from small range to big range?
  • CPU与内存之间的各级cache，由于访问时间的限制，完全由硬件电路实现，其实就是一个硬件哈希表
No.Concept
1A block is a fixed-sized packet of information that moves back and forth between a cache and main memory (or a lower-level cache).
2A line is a container in a cache that stores a block, as well as other information such as the valid bit and the tag bits.
3A set is a collection of one or more lines. Sets in direct-mapped caches consist of a single line. Sets in set associative and fully associative caches consist of multiple lines.

Chapter 7: Linking

  • 区分weak symbol与strong symbol，这会引起很难发现的bug
No.Rule
1Multiple strong symbols are not allowed
2Given a strong symbol and multiple weak symbols, choose the strong symbol
3Given multiple weak symbols, choose any of the weak symbols
  • 链接器的主要作用：symbol resolution与relocation

Chapter 8: Exceptional Control Flow

  • 异常控制流，分类与梳理
ClassCauseAsync/SyncReturn behavior
InterruptSignal from I/O deviceAsyncAlways returns to next instruction
TrapIntentional exceptionSyncAlways returns to next instruction
FaultPotentially recoverable errorSyncMight return to current instruction
AbortNonrecoverable errorSyncNever returns
  • 区分概念：parallel flows与concurrent flows，前者是后者的子集
  • 梳理概念：进程的若干状态
StatusDescription
RunningThe process is either executing on the CPU or is waiting to be executed and will eventually be scheduled by the kernel
StoppedThe execution of the process is suspended and will not be scheduled
TerminatedThe process is stopped permanently
  • 僵尸进程：a terminated process that has not yet been reaped is called a zombie
  • 信号量理解：
    • 定义一：higher-level software form of exceptional control flow
    • 定义二：signals are the software analog of hardware interrupts
  • 思考信号量实际使用场景中的坑？
  • 继续读书shell实现，rand检测

Chapter 9: Virtual Memory

  • 研究简化的case study，最利于理解复杂的概念
  • 理解memory mapping为什么给很多任务都能提供便利
  • DRAM caches are fully associative
  • 结合mmap再次理解fork，execve等系统调用

Chapter 10: System-Level I/O

  • 理解Linux的I/O层次：内核提供什么接口？用户态利用内核接口可以额外做哪些事情？
ClassContent
High-LevelStandard I/O functions are implemented “on top of” (using) the Unix I/O functions
Low-LevelUnix I/O is implemented in the operating system kernel
  • 思考一下short counts问题为什么会发生？
  • 理解linux内核如何维护open file，这跟file share关系密切
  • 10.9 Putting It Together: Which I/O Functions Should I Use? 重读，理清概念
